cards.front: What are the positions of primary hemorrhoids?
Why is internal hemorrhoid bleeding painless?
cards.back: Internal hemorrhoids are above dentate line — visceral innervation (no pain)

cards.front: What is the most effective office procedure for hemorrhoids?

cards.back: Open hemorrhoidectomy — wounds left open to heal by secondary intention
Stapled excision of rectal mucosa — reduces prolapse, less pain than conventional

cards.front: When should you suspect colorectal cancer with rectal bleeding?
cards.back: Age >40-50, change in bowel habit, weight loss, anemia, family history
Thrombosis in external hemorrhoidal plexus — acutely painful, may need excision

cards.front: What is the first-line treatment for Grade I-II hemorrhoids?
cards.back: Conservative (high fiber, fluids) + Rubber band ligation

cards.front: What are contraindications to rubber band ligation?

cards.back: Straining, constipation, pregnancy, prolonged sitting, low fiber diet
Painless bright red bleeding per rectum (on paper/in bowl)
